chunk_summary: This section discusses nutrition adaptations in cystic fibrosis (CF) due to long-term complications and CFTR modulator therapies, emphasizing reassessment of calorie and enzyme needs, proactive CFRD screening, and monitoring for bone and liver disease.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of CFTR modulators on nutrition and fat absorption in CF patients.
Learn the importance of proactive CFRD screening and appropriate management strategies.
Identify key nutritional considerations for bone and liver disease in CF patients.
Develop strategies for reassessing and adjusting calorie and enzyme needs in CF care.
key_facts: CFTR modulators can improve appetite, reduce exacerbations, and enhance fat absorption.
Reassess calorie and PERT plans 3–6 months after CFTR modulator initiation, especially if BMI exceeds target range.
Annual OGTT screening for CFRD is recommended starting at age 10, or earlier with risk factors like steroid use or pregnancy.
Fat-soluble vitamin levels should be rechecked as absorption may improve with CFTR modulators.
Ensure vitamin D and calcium adequacy, and consider DXA scans in high-risk older children for bone health.
Coordinate insulin strategies with overnight tube feeds in CFRD to preserve growth and lean mass.
reference_quote: CFTR modulators often improve appetite, reduce exacerbations, and may improve fat absorption.
document_key_topics: Cystic fibrosis-related diabetes (CFRD) screening and management
Bone and liver disease monitoring in CF
Reassessment of calorie and enzyme needs in CF care
